pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides an overview of the ESG Credits solution for financial institutions. Explains how the API enables ESG impact reporting for green investments, supporting multiple taxonomies, currencies, and languages with blockchain-based verification and double-counting prevention.
useCases: Banks creating digital ESG investor reports for customers with green asset portfolios
Investment advisors providing impact reports for broad customer base in digital channels
Institutional investors viewing consolidated portfolio impact reports for green assets
Venture capitalists providing sponsors with project-level and consolidated ESG reports
Issuers of green assets sharing impact details for promotional and calculation purposes
Certification bodies digitally publishing quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects

implementationSteps.title: Define Taxonomy
implementationSteps.description: Set up impact categories, indicators, and units using ICMA framework or custom taxonomy with support for two levels of categories and indicators

implementationSteps.title: Register Green Frameworks
implementationSteps.description: Create impact profiles for green frameworks, link UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives, and configure multi-currency support

implementationSteps.title: Calculate and Report
implementationSteps.description: Generate impact calculations per annum or per instrument term, create blockchain certificates, and prevent double-counting across the debt capital community

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right Solution Overview ESG Credits API chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right ESG Credits Solution Overview BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include: Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market. Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets. Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix. Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations. Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets. By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting. Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit. Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework. You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable. The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community. Previous ESG Credits chevron-left Next ESG Credits API chevron-right Last updated 2 months ago sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview

This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include:
Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market.
Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets.
Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix.
Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations.
Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets.

By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting.

Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit.

Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework.

You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable.

The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community.

pages.title: Calendar | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Calendar API for FAN Game Cube, representing scheduled games, matches, or tournament instances. Enables creation, listing, and deletion of time-bound events associated with specific fields for season-based or tournament-based fan campaigns.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new calendar for a sports season or tournament
Lists all calendars or retrieves a specific calendar by ID
Response containing the newly created calendar ID

useCases: Create season calendars for LaLiga 2025-2026 with multiple matches
Schedule UEFA Champions League Final as single event calendar
Manage tournament timelines for fan engagement campaigns
Link time-bound events to specific sports fields
Organize game-based or season-based fan participation

implementationSteps.title: Create Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Call new_calendar operation with calendar name (e.g., '2025-26' or 'Champions League Final') to receive a calendarId

implementationSteps.title: Associate with Field
implementationSteps.description: Use the returned calendarId to link scheduled games/matches to specific Field objects for gameplay tracking

implementationSteps.title: Manage Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Use list_calendars to view all calendars or delete_calendar to remove obsolete schedules

The Calendar object anchors the gameplay timeline, enabling season-based, game-based, or tournament-based fan campaigns. Examples: UEFA Champions League Final : Madrid vs PSG, for a single event calendar. LaLiga 2025-2026 , including multiple matches, for the entire season calendar. hashtag new_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Calendar name (e.g. LaLiga 2025-26) hashtag new_calendar API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( ' axios ' ) ; /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { " solution " : " BWS.NFT.GameCube " , " operation " : " new_calendar " , " parameters " : { " name " : " 2025-26 " } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : ' post ' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : ' https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call ' , headers : { ' X-Api-Key ' : ' XqaLg...... A5k2V729v ' , /* use your API key here! */ ' Content-Type ' : ' application/json ' }, data : JSON . stringify ( request ) } ; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console . log ( JSON . stringify ( response . data )) ; } ) .catch((error) => { console . log ( error ) ; } ); hashtag new_calendar Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the calendarId , which will be used to link with related objects. hashtag list_calendars POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters hashtag list_calendars Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string Calendar Id hashtag list_calendars API Call Example list_calendars API call using Node.js hashtag list_calendars Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of calendars you created or a single calendar if the CalendarId parameter is included in the call. hashtag delete_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption calendarId string Then Calendar Id of the calendar to delete. hashtag delete_calendar API Call Example delete_calendar API call using Node.js hashtag delete_calendar Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Cubes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Cubes API representing zones or tiles within a Field. Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by fans, automatically generated when creating fields. Includes operations to list cubes with pagination and update cube prices in USD cents.

codeExamples.description: Lists cubes for a field with pagination support
Updates pricing for specific cubes to $5.00 USD each
Example cube object with position coordinates and metadata

useCases: Sell NFT field zones to fans for specific sports venues
Track cube ownership for reward distribution during matches
Set dynamic pricing for premium field positions
Enable fans to own tokenized sections of famous stadiums
Manage hundreds of NFT cubes per field with pagination

implementationSteps.title: Cubes Auto-Generation
implementationSteps.description: Cubes are automatically created when a new Field is established, divided according to fieldLength, fieldWidth, and cubeLength parameters

implementationSteps.title: List and Query Cubes
implementationSteps.description: Use list_cubes with fieldId and limit parameters, utilizing lastEvaluatedKey for pagination through large cube collections

implementationSteps.title: Set Cube Pricing
implementationSteps.description: Call update_cube-price with fieldId, cubesList (using i_j index format), and priceInCents (e.g., 500 for $5.00)

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Cube API Cubes Represents a zone or tile within a Field . Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. triangle-exclamation Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field. hashtag list_cubes POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_cubes parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. limit number The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes). lastEvaluatedKey (optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes. hashtag list_cubes API Call Example list_cubes API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( ' axios ' ) ; /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { " solution " : " BWS.NFT.GameCube " , " operation " : " list_cubes " , " parameters " : { " fieldId " : " 5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2 " , " limit " : 5 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : ' post ' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : ' https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call ' , headers : { ' X-Api-Key ' : ' XqaLg...... A5k2V729v ' , /* use your API key here! */ ' Content-Type ' : ' application/json ' }, data : JSON . stringify ( request ) } ; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console . log ( JSON . stringify ( response . data )) ; } ) .catch((error) => { console . log ( error ) ; } ); hashtag list_cubes Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns a list of cubes and their related cube data, including the cubeId . If more cubes are available, the field lastEvaluatedKey is returned (include it on the API call for getting the next chunk of cubes). hashtag update_cube-price POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_cube-price parameters JSON check method parameters hashtag update_cube-price Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id cubesList list A list of cubes whose price needs to be updated. Use cube data i and j indexes to identify each cube using the format i_j (e.g. 40_0). priceInCents number Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price) hashtag update_cube-price API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag update_cube-price Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the number of correctly updated cubes, the number of invalid detected cubes, and the list of invalid cubes. check method parameters

pages.title: Event Types | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Event Types API for defining in-game occurrences like goals, assists, or passes. Events are registered with spatial coordinates and point values, affecting cube owners whose zones match the action location during live matches.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new event type 'pass' worth 100 points
Updates event point value from 100 to 1000 points
Response containing the newly created event type ID

useCases: Define scoring rules for different match events (goals, passes, assists)
Assign point values to cube owners based on field action locations
Create custom event types for different sports (goals, baskets, touchdowns)
Update event scoring dynamically during seasons
Reward fans whose cubes correspond to significant game actions

implementationSteps.title: Create Event Types
implementationSteps.description: Use new_event-type with fieldId, eventTypeName (e.g., 'pass', 'goal'), and eventTypePoints to establish scoring rules

implementationSteps.title: Configure Point Values
implementationSteps.description: Set appropriate point values for different event types based on significance (e.g., goals=1000, passes=100)

implementationSteps.title: Manage Event Types
implementationSteps.description: Use list_event-types to view all events, update_event-type to adjust points, or delete_event-type to remove obsolete event definitions

pages.title: Field | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Field API representing virtual sports fields tokenized into interactive zones (CUBES). Fields serve as the foundational canvas for gameplay, typically linked to real-world sports venues with configurable dimensions and cube sizes.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new field for Camp Nou stadium with 120x80 meters divided into 3x3 meter cubes
Retrieves details for a specific field by fieldId
Response containing the newly created field ID used for linking cubes and events

useCases: Tokenize famous stadiums like Real Madrid Stadium or Camp Nou into NFT zones
Create virtual representations of Madison Square Garden for basketball games
Configure field dimensions in centimeters for precise spatial event tracking
Automatically generate cube grids based on field and cube size specifications
Link real-world sports matches to virtual tokenized playing fields

implementationSteps.title: Create Field
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field with name, sport, fieldLength, fieldWidth (in centimeters), and cubeLength to define the tokenized playing area

implementationSteps.title: Cube Auto-Generation
implementationSteps.description: All cubes for the field are automatically created upon field creation, divided according to specified dimensions

implementationSteps.title: Manage Fields
implementationSteps.description: Use list_fields to view all fields or delete_field to remove fields (note: deleting a field also deletes all associated cubes)

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Cube API Field Represents a virtual sports field tokenized into interactive zones or sections ("CUBES"). It is the foundational canvas for all game interactions. Each field is typically linked to a real-world sports match. Examples: Real Madrid Stadium Madison Square Garden hashtag new_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_field parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Field name (e.g. Camp Nou) sport string Sport (e.g. Football) fieldLength number Field length in centimeters (e.g. 12000) fieldWidth number Field width in centimeters (e.g. 9000) cubeLength number The square size of a single cube (e.g. 300 for a cube of 3x3 meters) hashtag new_field API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag new_field Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the fieldId , which will be used to link NFT Game Cube related objects. All the cubes for that field are created when creating a new field. hashtag list_fields POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters hashtag list_fields Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id hashtag list_fields API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag list_fields Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of fields you created or a single field if fieldId parameter is included in the call. hashtag delete_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_field parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id to delete. hashtag delete_field API Call Example delete_field API call using Node.js hashtag delete_field Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. circle-info All the cubes created and assigned to the deleted field are also deleted. pages.title: Live Events | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Live Events API for recording in-match events at specific coordinates during scheduled matches. Serves as the primary mechanism for capturing game data like goals and plays, mapping them to corresponding cubes for scoring and rewards.

codeExamples.description: Registers a live event at coordinates (45, 40) during a match for cube scoring
Successful event registration response

useCases: Record goals with spatial coordinates during live soccer matches
Capture chess moves automatically via AI-driven recognition
Register passes, fouls, and plays at specific field locations
Power fan engagement through real-time event-based cube rewards
Enable manual event entry via organizer dashboard for smaller events
Integrate machine learning models for automatic game event detection

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Match Context
implementationSteps.description: Gather matchId, fieldId, and calendarId to identify which scheduled match on which field the event occurs

implementationSteps.title: Define Event Details
implementationSteps.description: Specify event coordinates (x, y) and eventTypeId to indicate what type of action occurred at which location

implementationSteps.title: Register Event
implementationSteps.description: Call new_event API with all parameters to record the event and trigger cube-based scoring for affected zone owners

It is the primary mechanism by which game data—such as goals, moves, fouls, or plays—is captured and mapped to corresponding Cubes for scoring and reward processing. This API serves as a trusted interface to power fan engagement: Manually via the organizer dashboard or solution UI (BWS Solution Interface) Automatically via BWS-provided machine learning models that detect and push game events in real time (e.g., AI-driven goal recognition, chess move capture) Through your application. hashtag new_event POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_event parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_event Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id for which you want to register the event. fieldId string The Field Id the match is linked to. calendarId string The calendar Id the match is linked to. event JSON check event parameter hashtag new_event Event Parameter Parameter Type Desciption coordinates JSON check coordinates parameter eventTypeId string The Event Type Id of the event you want to register. hashtag new_event Coordinates Parameter Parameter Type Desciption x string The 2D x coordinate of the event. y string The 2D y coordinate of the event. hashtag new_event API Call Example new_event API call using Node.js hashtag new_event Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Match | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Match API endpoints for creating, updating, listing, and deleting time-bound gameplay sessions in the FAN Game Cube platform. Matches represent game instances like football matches or chess rounds, scheduled via Calendar objects and hosted on defined Fields with event tracking and fan interactions.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new match in the FAN Game Cube system with team details, start time, and match image
Lists matches for a specific calendar with optional filtering by match ID
Successful API response returning the matchId for newly created or updated matches

useCases: Create football match events like PSG vs Manchester City
Schedule chess tournament rounds between players
Track sports game sessions with team flags and descriptions
Manage match status through lifecycle (scheduled, playing, finished, canceled)
Set pricing for match-specific cube purchases in USD cents

implementationSteps.title: Create Match
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match operation with calendarId, startTimeInMillis, match name, description, team details, and base64 encoded images for match announcement

implementationSteps.title: Update Match Details
implementationSteps.description: Call update_match with matchId and calendarId, providing only the fields you want to update such as status, description, or pricing

implementationSteps.title: List and Delete Matches
implementationSteps.description: Use list_matches to retrieve match details with IPFS image URLs, or delete_match to remove matches by matchId and calendarId

It represents a game instance—such as a football match, chess round, or tournament heat—scheduled via the Calendar object and hosted on a defined Field .\ Examples: PSG vs. Manchester City Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura circle-info Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. hashtag new_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_match Method Parameters circle-info Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and startTimeInMillis). Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match should be included in. startTimeInMillis * number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled". priceInCents number The default price in USD cents for all the cubes if no specific price is defined for a cube (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD). hashtag new_match API Call Example new_match API call using Node.js hashtag new_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the newly created match. hashtag update_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_match parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag update_match Method Parameters circle-info Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and matchId) Only provide those you want to update. Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match is included in. matchId * string The Match Id we want to update. startTimeInMillis number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled" priceInCents number The price in USD cents (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD) hashtag update_match API Call Example update_match API call using Node.js hashtag update_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the updated match. hashtag list_matches POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_matches parameters JSON check method parameters hashtag list_matches Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. matchId string (optional) The Match Id we want to list. hashtag list_matches API Call Example list_matches API call using Node.js hashtag list_matches Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matches for the provided calendar. If we give a Match Id, it will just return the match we want to list. hashtag delete_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag delete_match Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id of the match we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. hashtag delete_match API Call Example delete_match API call using Node.js hashtag delete_match Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Plays (Field-Calendar) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Details the Field-Calendar relationship API (Plays) which models the temporal lifecycle of sports fields in the NFT Game Cube solution. Fields define static spatial configurations while Calendar entries dynamically represent individual matches, tournaments, or rounds on that field.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new field-calendar relationship linking a sports field to a tournament or season calendar
Lists all calendars associated with a specific field ID
Response showing field-calendar relationship with IPFS stored image URL

useCases: Create PSG Champions League 2026 season on specific stadium field
Manage New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season calendar
Link tournament schedules to physical sports venues
Track multiple seasons on the same sports field
Associate calendar events with field spatial configurations

implementationSteps.title: Create Field-Calendar Link
implementationSteps.description: Use new_field-calendar with fieldId, calendarId, description (e.g. 'PSG 2025-26'), and base64 encoded season/tournament image

implementationSteps.title: List Field Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Call list_field-calendars with fieldId to retrieve all calendar relationships and their IPFS image URLs

implementationSteps.title: Delete Relationship
implementationSteps.description: Remove field-calendar links using delete_field-calendar with both fieldId and calendarId parameters

pages.title: Prizes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Prizes API for creating, updating, listing, and deleting reward assets in the FAN Game Cube platform. Prizes represent rewards like signed merchandise, VIP tickets, or bonus tokens that users can receive based on their cube performance in matches or seasons.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new first-place prize for a match with name, description, and base64 encoded image
Lists all prizes configured for a specific match ID
Successful response returning the prizeId for newly created prizes

useCases: Create signed merchandise prizes for match winners
Offer VIP match tickets as tournament rewards
Set up Zoom call or AMA prizes with athletes
Distribute bonus BWS tokens to top performers
Configure first, second, and third place prizes for matches

implementationSteps.title: Create Match Prize
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match-prize with matchId, calendarId, position (1-3), prize name, description, and snapshot image in base64 encoding

implementationSteps.title: Update Prize Details
implementationSteps.description: Call update_match-prize with prizeId to modify prize attributes like htmlDescription or additional images (image1, image2, image3)

implementationSteps.title: List and Delete Prizes
implementationSteps.description: Use list_match-prizes to retrieve all prizes with IPFS URLs, or delete_match-prize with prizeId to remove specific prizes

pages.title: FAN Game Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides comprehensive overview of the FAN Game Cube platform, explaining how it revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own NFT-based digital assets linked to specific field sections. Covers multi-sport scalability, pricing strategies, revenue potential, NFT ownership, trading mechanics, and real-time engagement features across football, chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, and other sports.
useCases: Tokenize football stadium sections into 500-2000 cubes for match engagement
Create chess tournament experiences with 64 chessboard square NFTs
Enable tennis fans to own baseline and service box digital assets
Divide cricket fields into batting zones and boundary sections for fan interaction
Generate revenue through NBA basketball court cube sales (three-point arc, paint zone)
Launch weekend chess tournament NFT Game Cube events

implementationSteps.title: Define Cube Allocation
implementationSteps.description: Determine how many cubes to sell based on sport (e.g., 500-2000 for football, 64 for chess) and divide playing area into strategic zones with premium pricing for high-value locations

implementationSteps.title: Configure Pricing Strategy
implementationSteps.description: Set dynamic pricing for prime field areas (higher cost), secondary zones (lower cost like $10), and implement pricing models based on match importance and demand

implementationSteps.title: Deploy Purchase Widget
implementationSteps.description: Integrate one-click JavaScript widget into club website for seamless cube purchases using standard credit cards, enabling fans to buy, manage, and trade cubes through mobile app

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview Building New Revenue Streams for the Sports Industry BWS's FAN Game Cube platform revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own digital assets linked to specific sections of a sports field. This extends beyond football to include chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, and any sport with a field that can be split into "cubes". As real-time events unfold in their selected area, fans earn points and unlock exciting rewards, all while creating new revenue opportunities for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and other key figures in the sports industry. Want to launch a FAN Game Cube event? Contact us here arrow-up-right . hashtag Cube Allocation for Different Sports Sports organizers can define how many cubes to sell and how to divide their playing area based on the sport’s unique characteristics. A football/soccer field cubes division. Examples include: Football stadiums It is typically divided into 500, 1000, or 2000 cubes , ensuring key areas like penalty boxes and midfield zones hold premium value. Chess tournaments The 64 chessboard squares can be tokenized, offering fans a unique way to interact with their favorite chess events. Tennis courts Tokenizing the baseline, service boxes, and net area allows fans to engage in real-time match events. Cricket fields Dividing the pitch into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections enhances fan participation. Basketball courts Cube allocation can include key areas like the three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone . Rugby fields Fans can purchase cubes covering try zones, scrums, and midfield sections . Baseball fields Key locations such as bases, outfield zones, and pitcher’s mound can be tokenized. And a long etcetera. hashtag NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading FAN Game Cube utilizes NFT technology to ensure verifiable ownership. However, the usage of NFT goes beyond ownership, allowing fans to trade their cubes as they would with traditional assets. During a season or tournament, cube owners can buy and sell cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces, generating additional royalties for sports clubs and organizers. Since NFTs offer permanent and transparent ownership records , fans gain long-term value while clubs benefit from secondary sales. hashtag Pricing and Monetization Strategy Organizers have complete control over pricing, ensuring dynamic valuation based on strategic cube placement. Different pricing strategies include: Prime field areas Higher prices can be charged for high-demand sections, such as the center of a football pitch, penalty spots, or key cricket fielding positions. Secondary zones To encourage broad participation, less strategic locations, such as side areas or backfield positions, can be offered at $10 or lower. Dynamic pricing models Pricing can fluctuate based on match importance, demand, and historical engagement levels. hashtag Revenue Potential Your expected revenue is calculated as follows: Number of cubes × Average Price per cube = Total income For example, launching 500 cubes at an average price of $500 per cube on a VIP event can generate $250,000 in revenue. Larger stadiums, premium tournaments, or full-season FAN Game Cubes can scale this model to generate even higher revenues. hashtag Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget BWS provides a one-click purchase widget for frictionless transactions. Sports clubs do not need to create special accounts or navigate complex payment gateways—cubes can be sold in just a few hours. A weekend Chess Tournament NFT Game Cube event. The widget integrates easily into existing club websites, allowing fans to purchase cubes as simply as buying a regular game ticket. hashtag How Fans Can Participate and Win Once a sports club publishes its one-click buy widget , fans can easily purchase FAN Game Cubes using standard credit cards, making participation straightforward and accessible. One-click FAN Game Cube purchase experience This experience brings a new layer of engagement to the fan experience while giving them direct ownership of in-game digital assets. hashtag Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics NFT-based cubes offer fans an engaging way to experience their favorite sports, providing: True ownership of digital sports assets. Fans can buy, sell, or hold tradeable assets for future value (full-season FAN Game Cube). Passive earnings potential through resale royalties and long-term value accumulation. Access to real-time engagement through a dedicated mobile app. Once a cube is purchased, fans can: Manage their cube collection across multiple sports. Track upcoming matches in real time. Join live leaderboards to compete with other fans for top prizes. hashtag Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Multi-Sport Scalability Works across various sports, including football, cricket, chess, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, golf, Formula 1, and esports. It is flexible and adaptable to different event structures and fan bases. One-Click Buy Widget A JavaScript widget for effortless integration that enhances the club’s purchasing experience with quick, seamless transactions. Easy & Free to Launch Clubs and organizers can set up Fan Game Cube events using a simple three-step workflow or API calls. Flexible Event Calendars Supports single VIP games, full-season matches, and tournaments. It is scalable, from small local events like the Ohio Chess Tournament to major leagues like NBA teams. Real-Time Fan Engagement A comprehensive mobile app enables clubs to foster deep connections with fans during matches. Features include live-streaming for real-time game viewing and interactive chat to enhance fan engagement and discussion. BWS Fan Game Cube is the future of sports engagement, bridging the digital and real-world experiences for fans while unlocking new revenue opportunities for sports organizations. Get started today and transform the way fans interact with their favorite games! Generate automated performance reports for community analytics

pages.summary: Complete command reference page listing all 73 X Bot commands organized by 12 functional categories. Provides navigation to detailed documentation for filter setup, basic commands, advanced filtering, project configuration, raid commands, schedule management, reports, leaderboard customization, topic management, admin functions, and subscription management.
useCases: Configure tracking filters for accounts, keywords, and cashtags
Set up automated report generation schedules
Customize engagement scoring with point multipliers
Manage complex tracking scenarios with named filters
Start and monitor engagement raids on X posts
Configure project metadata including logos and URLs

implementationSteps.title: First Time Setup
implementationSteps.description: Run /start to initialize, /add_accounts to track X accounts, /set_project_name to configure project, and /recreate to generate first report

implementationSteps.title: Daily Operations
implementationSteps.description: Use /report for latest analytics, /raidx to start engagement raids, and /raid_status to check active raid progress

implementationSteps.title: Advanced Configuration
implementationSteps.description: Apply /set_x_filtering for complex tracking, /set_schedule for automated reports, and /set_points to customize scoring

Initialize the bot for your group /add_accounts arrow-up-right - Add X accounts to track /set_project_name arrow-up-right - Configure project name /recreate arrow-up-right - Generate your first analytics report hashtag Daily Operations /report arrow-up-right - View latest performance analytics /raidx arrow-up-right - Start a new engagement raid /raid_status arrow-up-right - Check active raid progress hashtag Advanced Configuration /set_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Create named filters for complex tracking /set_schedule arrow-up-right - Automate report generation /set_points arrow-up-right - Customize engagement scoring hashtag All Commands by Category hashtag Filter Setup (20 commands) Manage tracking filters for accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignore lists. Commands: /list_filters arrow-up-right - Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each. /show_filter arrow-up-right - Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags,. /add_accounts arrow-up-right - Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. /add_keywords arrow-up-right - Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. /add_cashtags arrow-up-right - Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. /add_mentions arrow-up-right - Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. /add_excludes arrow-up-right - Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. /add_ignore arrow-up-right - Adds X accounts to ignore. /remove_accounts arrow-up-right - Removes specific X accounts from tracking. /remove_keywords arrow-up-right - Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. /remove_cashtags arrow-up-right - Removes specific cashtags from tracking. /remove_mentions arrow-up-right - Removes X handles from mention tracking. /remove_excludes arrow-up-right - Removes keywords from the exclusion list. /remove_ignore arrow-up-right - Removes X accounts from the ignore list. /clear_accounts arrow-up-right - Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. /clear_keywords arrow-up-right - Removes ALL keywords from the filter. /clear_cashtags arrow-up-right - Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. /clear_mentions arrow-up-right - Removes ALL mentions from the filter. /clear_excludes arrow-up-right - Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. /clear_ignore arrow-up-right - Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. hashtag Basic Commands (4 commands) Essential bot operations including help, initialization, and status checks. Commands: /help arrow-up-right - Displays a complete list of available bot commands and their descriptions. /start arrow-up-right - Initializes the bot and provides welcome messages. /status arrow-up-right - Checks if the bot is operational and running properly in your group. /get_chatid arrow-up-right - Displays your Telegram group's unique chat ID. hashtag Advanced Filtering (3 commands) Create named filters for complex tracking scenarios and multi-project management. Commands: /set_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Creates advanced named filters with complex X API query syntax. /get_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. /delete_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. hashtag Project Configuration (10 commands) Customize project metadata including name, description, logo, and URLs for reports. Commands: /set_project_name arrow-up-right - Sets your project or community name that will appear in all generated reports, leaderboards, and on the. /get_project_name arrow-up-right - Displays the current project name configured for your community. /set_project_description arrow-up-right - Sets a short description of your project that appears in reports and on the website. /get_project_description arrow-up-right - Displays the current project description configured for your community. /set_project_long_description arrow-up-right - Sets a detailed, comprehensive description of your project for the website dashboard. /get_project_long_description arrow-up-right - Displays the current detailed project description configured for the website dashboard. /set_project_logo arrow-up-right - Uploads your project logo that will appear on reports and the website dashboard. /get_project_logo arrow-up-right - Displays the URL of your currently configured project logo. /set_project_urls arrow-up-right - Sets important links for your project including website, social media, documentation, and other resources. /get_project_urls arrow-up-right - Displays all project URLs currently configured for your community. hashtag Raid Commands (11 commands) Coordinate X (Twitter) engagement raids to amplify post visibility and reach. Commands: /raidx arrow-up-right - Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. /raid_status arrow-up-right - Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. /raid_stop arrow-up-right - Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. /raid_history arrow-up-right - Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. /set_raid_defaults arrow-up-right - Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior,. /get_raid_defaults arrow-up-right - Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration,. /set_raid_message_behavior arrow-up-right - Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. /get_raid_message_behavior arrow-up-right - Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. /set_raid_image arrow-up-right - Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. /auto_raid_config arrow-up-right - Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. /get_auto_raid_config arrow-up-right - Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after. hashtag Schedule Management (3 commands) Configure automated report generation schedules with custom intervals. Commands: /set_schedule arrow-up-right - Sets a schedule for when reports should be automatically generated. /get_schedule arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current scheduled time for automatic report generation. /delete_schedule arrow-up-right - Removes the configured daily schedule for automatic report generation. hashtag Calendar & Time (2 commands) Manage calendar settings and time periods for report generation cycles. Commands: /set_calendar arrow-up-right - Sets up a recurring calendar schedule for automatic report generation. /get_calendar arrow-up-right - Displays the current calendar schedule configuration for automatic reports, showing the start date and how often. hashtag Reports (2 commands) Generate and manage performance analytics reports for tracked X content. Commands: /report arrow-up-right - Displays the most recently generated leaderboard report for your community. /recreate arrow-up-right - Triggers immediate generation of a fresh report by fetching latest X (Twitter) data and creating new leaderboards. hashtag Leaderboard Customization (10 commands) Customize scoring points, colors, titles, and display settings for leaderboard reports. Commands: /set_points arrow-up-right - Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_points arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics. /set_colors arrow-up-right - Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_colors arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics. /set_top_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. /set_best_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. /set_engagement_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. /get_titles arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including. /set_top_count arrow-up-right - Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. /get_top_count arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report. hashtag Topic Management (2 commands) Configure Telegram topic/thread settings for bot operation. Commands: /set_topic arrow-up-right - Configures which forum topic (thread) the bot should send reports to in Telegram groups that use forum-style topics. /get_topic arrow-up-right - Displays which forum topic (thread) is currently configured to receive bot reports. hashtag Admin (1 commands) Administrative functions for managing bot notifications and permissions. Commands: /add_admin arrow-up-right - Designates specific group administrators to receive important bot notifications via private messages, such as. hashtag Subscription & Credits (5 commands) Manage billing, subscriptions, credits, and usage tracking. Commands: /subscription arrow-up-right - Provides access to Stripe customer portal for subscription management including billing history,. /buy arrow-up-right - Initiates the purchase process for X Bot Professional subscription. /get_credits arrow-up-right - Displays the current credit usage and availability for the bot. /payment_success arrow-up-right - Handles successful payment confirmation after Stripe checkout completion. /payment_cancelled arrow-up-right - Handles payment cancellation when users exit Stripe checkout without completing payment. pages.title: Admin | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the /add_admin command for designating specific group administrators to receive bot notifications via private messages. This admin-only command helps limit alerts about credit exhaustion, subscription renewals, and errors to specific admins who manage the bot, reducing notification noise for other administrators.

codeExamples.description: Designates multiple Telegram users as bot admins to receive notification alerts
Clears all designated admin users from receiving bot notifications

useCases: Limit bot notifications to specific group administrators
Receive credit exhaustion warnings in private messages
Get subscription renewal alerts for bot management
Reduce notification noise for non-bot-managing admins
Configure error alert recipients for troubleshooting

By default, the bot sends notifications to all group admins, but you can use this command to limit notifications to specific admins who manage the bot. This helps reduce notification noise for admins who don't need bot alerts. All designated users must already be group administrators. paragraphs: Designates specific group administrators to receive important bot notifications via private messages, such as credit exhaustion warnings, subscription renewals, or error alerts. By default, the bot sends notifications to all group admins, but you can use this command to limit notifications to specific admins who manage the bot. This helps reduce notification noise for admins who don't need bot alerts. All designated users must already be group administrators.

pages.title: Advanced Filtering | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Details advanced filtering commands for creating sophisticated X tracking queries using Boolean logic and special operators. Includes /set_x_filtering for complex query syntax with OR/AND operators, /get_x_filtering to list configured filters, and /delete_x_filtering to remove named filters beyond simple add/remove commands.

codeExamples.description: Creates a named filter tracking mentions of a project handle or hashtag using OR logic
Sets up filter tracking specific accounts posting about BWS keywords
Creates filter for trending hashtags while excluding spam content and specific accounts

useCases: Track multiple KOL accounts posting about specific keywords
Monitor project mentions across handles and hashtags
Filter trending topics while excluding spam content
Create complex multi-criteria tracking scenarios
Combine account tracking with keyword filtering using Boolean logic
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Advanced Filtering hashtag /set_x_filtering Creates advanced named filters with complex X API query syntax. Supports combining multiple criteria using logical operators (OR, AND) and special operators (from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, ignore:). Use this for sophisticated tracking scenarios requiring multiple conditions and Boolean logic that go beyond simple add/remove commands. Admin Only Copy /set_x_filtering project_mentions mention:@MyProject OR #MyProjectToken Copy /set_x_filtering my_kols from:@CryptoHayes OR from:@IncomeSharks keywords:BWS Copy /set_x_filtering trending #DeFi OR #crypto OR #blockchain exclude:scam ignore:@SpamBot Parameters: Name Type Example filter_name required my_filter query_syntax required value hashtag /get_x_filtering Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. Shows each filter's name and its complete X API query syntax, helping you understand what content is being tracked. Use this to review your sophisticated filtering setup created with /set_x_filtering. Admin Only Copy /get_x_filtering hashtag /delete_x_filtering Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. Remove a specific filter by name to stop tracking that particular query, or use the wildcard "*" to delete all advanced filters at once. This does not affect simple filters managed with add/remove commands. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter_name required my_filter Previous Basic Commands chevron-left Next Project Configuration chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago /set_x_filtering /get_x_filtering /delete_x_filtering sun-bright desktop moon Copy /delete_x_filtering my_kols sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: Creates advanced named filters with complex X API query syntax. Supports combining multiple criteria using logical operators (OR, AND) and special operators (from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, ignore:). Use this for sophisticated tracking scenarios requiring multiple conditions and Boolean logic that go beyond simple add/remove commands.

Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. Shows each filter's name and its complete X API query syntax, helping you understand what content is being tracked. Use this to review your sophisticated filtering setup created with /set_x_filtering.

Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. Remove a specific filter by name to stop tracking that particular query, or use the wildcard "*" to delete all advanced filters at once. This does not affect simple filters managed with add/remove commands.

pages.title: Basic Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents four essential bot commands: /help for command lists, /start for initialization and welcome messages, /status for operational checks, and /get_chatid for retrieving Telegram group unique identifiers. These admin-only commands provide basic bot operations and troubleshooting capabilities.

codeExamples.description: Displays complete list of available commands with descriptions, showing different views for admins vs regular users
Initializes bot in group chats or shows welcome message with 'Add to Group' button in private messages
Checks if bot is operational and provides documentation link for setup instructions
Displays Telegram group's unique chat ID for technical configurations and support

useCases: Discover available bot commands and their descriptions
Initialize bot in new Telegram groups
Verify bot responsiveness after installation
Retrieve chat ID for API integrations
Troubleshoot bot connection issues
Complete subscription purchase confirmations
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Basic Commands hashtag /help Displays a complete list of available bot commands and their descriptions. Group administrators see all commands including advanced configuration options, while regular users see a limited list of basic commands they can use. Use this to discover what the bot can do and get quick command references. Admin Only Copy /help hashtag /start Initializes the bot and provides welcome messages. In group chats, confirms the bot is running and directs you to use /help for available commands. In private messages with the bot, shows a welcome message with an "Add to Group" button to install the bot in your Telegram group. Also handles payment confirmations when you complete a subscription purchase. Admin Only Copy /start hashtag /status Checks if the bot is operational and running properly in your group. Confirms the bot is active and provides a link to the documentation for setup instructions. Useful for verifying the bot is responsive after installation or troubleshooting connection issues. Admin Only Copy /status hashtag /get_chatid Displays your Telegram group's unique chat ID. This identifier is useful for technical configurations, integrations with other systems, or when contacting support. The chat ID is a unique number that Telegram assigns to each group and is required for certain advanced features and API integrations. Initializes the bot and provides welcome messages. In group chats, confirms the bot is running and directs you to use /help for available commands. Also handles payment confirmations when you complete a subscription purchase.

Checks if the bot is operational and running properly in your group. Confirms the bot is active and provides a link to the documentation for setup instructions. Useful for verifying the bot is responsive after installation or troubleshooting connection issues.

pages.title: Calendar & Time | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Details calendar and time management commands for automated report generation. Includes /set_calendar for configuring recurring schedules with start dates and cadence intervals, and /get_calendar for viewing current schedule configurations. All times operate in UTC timezone.

codeExamples.description: Sets up weekly automated reports starting June 20, 2025 with 7-day intervals
Displays current calendar schedule configuration showing start date and report generation cadence

useCases: Schedule weekly automated performance reports
Set up recurring analytics generation at specific intervals
Configure report start dates up to 6 days in the past
Verify current calendar schedule configuration
Automate regular community engagement tracking
Plan future report generation cycles
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Calendar & Time hashtag /set_calendar Sets up a recurring calendar schedule for automatic report generation. Specify a start date and how often reports should be generated (cadence in days). The bot will automatically create reports at the specified intervals starting from the chosen date. For example, setting "20/06/2025 7days" will generate weekly reports starting June 20, 2025. The start date can be up to 6 days in the past or any future date. All times are in UTC timezone. Admin Only Copy /set_calendar 20/06/2025 7days Parameters: Name Type Example start_date required 20/06/2025 cadence required 7days hashtag /get_calendar Displays the current calendar schedule configuration for automatic reports, showing the start date and how often reports are generated (cadence). Use this to verify your report generation schedule or to check if a calendar has been configured yet. All times are shown in UTC timezone. Admin Only Copy /get_calendar Previous Schedule Management chevron-left Next Reports chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago /set_calendar /get_calendar sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: Sets up a recurring calendar schedule for automatic report generation. Specify a start date and how often reports should be generated (cadence in days). The bot will automatically create reports at the specified intervals starting from the chosen date. For example, setting "20/06/2025 7days" will generate weekly reports starting June 20, 2025. The start date can be up to 6 days in the past or any future date. All times are in UTC timezone.

Displays the current calendar schedule configuration for automatic reports, showing the start date and how often reports are generated (cadence). Use this to verify your report generation schedule or to check if a calendar has been configured yet. All times are shown in UTC timezone.

pages.title: Filter Setup | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive documentation for X Bot filter configuration commands. Covers viewing, adding, removing, and clearing filters for tracking X (Twitter) accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. All commands are admin-only and support named filters for organizing different tracking scenarios.

codeExamples.description: Adds X (Twitter) accounts to track for monitoring posts and engagement
Adds keywords to a named filter for tracking specific topics in X posts
Adds exclusion keywords to filter out unwanted content from reports

useCases: Track crypto project team members and influencers separately using named filters
Monitor multiple cryptocurrency token cashtags for market sentiment analysis
Filter out spam and bot accounts while tracking legitimate community engagement
Track mentions of project founders and key accounts across X conversations
Organize KOL tracking by categories like team, influencers, and competitors

implementationSteps.title: Add Accounts to Track
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_accounts command with X handles to monitor posts from specific accounts. Optionally specify a named filter to organize different account groups.

implementationSteps.title: Configure Keywords and Cashtags
implementationSteps.description: Add relevant keywords with /add_keywords and token symbols with /add_cashtags to track topic-specific conversations and cryptocurrency mentions.

implementationSteps.title: Set Up Exclusions
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_excludes and /add_ignore to filter out spam, unwanted content, and irrelevant accounts from your tracking results.

implementationSteps.title: Review Filter Configuration
implementationSteps.description: Run /list_filters to see all configured filters and /show_filter to view detailed configuration including the resulting X API query.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Filter Setup hashtag View Filters hashtag /list_filters Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each filter tracks. Useful for managing multiple filters and understanding your current tracking setup. Admin Only Copy /list_filters hashtag /show_filter Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. Shows the resulting X API query that will be used for tracking. Admin Only Copy /show_filter Copy /show_filter filter=kols Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional kols hashtag Add Filters hashtag /add_accounts Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. Posts from these accounts will be monitored and included in reports and leaderboards. You can optionally specify a named filter to organize different account groups (e.g., team members, influencers, competitors). Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @CryptoHayes filter optional team hashtag /add_keywords Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. Any post containing these keywords will be monitored and included in reports. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Keywords can include hashtags. Helps filter content by topics, brands, or specific terms relevant to your project. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required crypto filter optional trending hashtag /add_cashtags Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. Any post containing these cashtags will be monitored and included in reports. Cashtags must start with $ symbol. Useful for tracking cryptocurrency tokens, stock symbols, or project-specific ticker symbols. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example cashtags required $BWS filter optional tokens hashtag /add_mentions Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. Any post that mentions these accounts (using @handle) will be monitored and included in reports. Different from add_accounts which tracks posts FROM accounts - this tracks posts ABOUT/MENTIONING accounts. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mentions required value filter optional influencers hashtag /add_excludes Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. Posts containing these excluded terms will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to remove noise, spam, or irrelevant content from your tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required spam filter optional clean hashtag /add_ignore Adds X accounts to ignore. Posts from these accounts will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to exclude bot accounts, spam accounts, or specific users whose posts you don't want to track. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @SpamBot filter optional clean hashtag Remove/Clear Filters hashtag /remove_accounts Removes specific X accounts from tracking. Posts from these accounts will no longer be monitored or included in reports. Use this to stop tracking accounts that are no longer relevant to your project. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @OldAccount filter optional kols hashtag /remove_keywords Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. Posts containing only these keywords will no longer be monitored. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required oldterm filter optional trending hashtag /remove_cashtags Removes specific cashtags from tracking. Posts containing only these cashtags will no longer be monitored. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example cashtags required $OLDTOKEN filter optional tokens hashtag /remove_mentions Removes X handles from mention tracking. Posts mentioning only these accounts will no longer be monitored. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mentions required value filter optional influencers hashtag /remove_excludes Removes keywords from the exclusion list. Posts containing these terms will no longer be automatically filtered out. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required spam filter optional clean hashtag /remove_ignore Removes X accounts from the ignore list. Posts from these accounts will now be tracked if they match filter criteria. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @ReinstatedAccount filter optional clean hashtag /clear_accounts Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. Use this to reset account tracking and start fresh. This does not delete the filter itself, only clears the accounts list. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional kols hashtag /clear_keywords Removes ALL keywords from the filter. Use this to reset keyword tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional trending hashtag /clear_cashtags Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. Use this to reset cashtag tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional tokens hashtag /clear_mentions Removes ALL mentions from the filter. Use this to reset mention tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional influencers hashtag /clear_excludes Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. Posts with previously excluded terms will now be tracked. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional clean hashtag /clear_ignore Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. Posts from previously ignored accounts will now be tracked.
