paragraphs: Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each filter tracks. Useful for managing multiple filters and understanding your current tracking setup.

Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. Shows the resulting X API query that will be used for tracking.

Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. Posts from these accounts will be monitored and included in reports and leaderboards. You can optionally specify a named filter to organize different account groups (e.g., team members, influencers, competitors).

Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. Any post containing these keywords will be monitored and included in reports. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Keywords can include hashtags. Helps filter content by topics, brands, or specific terms relevant to your project.

Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. Any post containing these cashtags will be monitored and included in reports. Cashtags must start with $ symbol. Useful for tracking cryptocurrency tokens, stock symbols, or project-specific ticker symbols.

Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. Any post that mentions these accounts (using @handle) will be monitored and included in reports. Different from add_accounts which tracks posts FROM accounts - this tracks posts ABOUT/MENTIONING accounts.

Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. Posts containing these excluded terms will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to remove noise, spam, or irrelevant content from your tracking.

Adds X accounts to ignore. Posts from these accounts will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to exclude bot accounts, spam accounts, or specific users whose posts you don't want to track.

Removes specific X accounts from tracking. Posts from these accounts will no longer be monitored or included in reports. Use this to stop tracking accounts that are no longer relevant to your project.
Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. Posts containing only these keywords will no longer be monitored. Use quotes for multi-word phrases.
Removes specific cashtags from tracking. Posts containing only these cashtags will no longer be monitored.
Removes X handles from mention tracking. Posts mentioning only these accounts will no longer be monitored.
Removes keywords from the exclusion list. Posts containing these terms will no longer be automatically filtered out.
Removes X accounts from the ignore list. Posts from these accounts will now be tracked if they match filter criteria.
Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. Use this to reset account tracking and start fresh. This does not delete the filter itself, only clears the accounts list.
Removes ALL keywords from the filter. Use this to reset keyword tracking.
Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. Use this to reset cashtag tracking.
Removes ALL mentions from the filter. Use this to reset mention tracking.
Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. Posts with previously excluded terms will now be tracked.
Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. Posts from previously ignored accounts will now be tracked.

pages.title: Leaderboard Customization | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot leaderboard customization commands. Explains how to configure scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, views), set custom colors for visual reports, customize report section titles, and control the number of users displayed in leaderboards. All commands are admin-only.

codeExamples.description: Sets scoring multipliers for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and views to calculate engagement scores
Configures HTML hex colors for displaying different engagement metrics in reports
Sets custom title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports

useCases: Weight replies higher than likes to encourage meaningful conversation in community competitions
Customize report colors to match project branding and visual identity
Configure scoring to prioritize retweets for campaigns focused on content amplification
Set custom leaderboard titles for weekly or monthly community engagement contests
Adjust top user count to feature more or fewer participants in competitive leaderboards

implementationSteps.title: Configure Engagement Scoring
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_points to define multipliers for each engagement type (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, views) based on your community's priorities.

implementationSteps.title: Customize Visual Styling
implementationSteps.description: Set brand-aligned colors with /set_colors and customize section titles using /set_top_title, /set_best_title, and /set_engagement_title.

implementationSteps.title: Adjust Leaderboard Display
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_top_count to control how many top-performing users appear in your generated reports and leaderboards.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Leaderboard Customization hashtag /set_points Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. These multipliers determine how many points each type of engagement contributes to a user's total score. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Copy /set_points 5 1 20 10 0.01 Parameters: Name Type Example likes required 50 retweets required 50 replies required 50 quotes required 50 views required value hashtag /get_points Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. Shows either configured values or indicates default values are being used. Copy /get_points hashtag /set_colors Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) in the leaderboard reports. Colors are stored as hexadecimal values and used for report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example likes required 50 retweets required 50 replies required 50 quotes required 50 views required value hashtag /get_colors Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard reports. Shows either configured colors or default values if none are set. hashtag /set_top_title Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. Allows customization of the primary heading displayed at the top of leaderboard reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /set_best_title Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above the highlighted best tweet. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /set_engagement_title Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above engagement scoring rules and metrics. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /get_titles Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including top title (main leaderboard), best title (best tweet), and engagement title (scoring rules). Shows either configured values or default values for each section. hashtag /set_top_count Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. This controls how many top-performing users are shown in the generated reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example count required value hashtag /get_top_count Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report leaderboard. Shows either the configured value or indicates default value (10) is being used. paragraphs: Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. These multipliers determine how many points each type of engagement contributes to a user's total score. Only group administrators can execute this command.

Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. Shows either configured values or indicates default values are being used.

Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) in the leaderboard reports. Colors are stored as hexadecimal values and used for report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command.

Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard reports. Shows either configured colors or default values if none are set.

Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. Allows customization of the primary heading displayed at the top of leaderboard reports. Only group administrators can execute this command.

Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above the highlighted best tweet. Only group administrators can execute this command.

Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above engagement scoring rules and metrics. Only group administrators can execute this command.

Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including top title (main leaderboard), best title (best tweet), and engagement title (scoring rules). Shows either configured values or default values for each section.
Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. This controls how many top-performing users are shown in the generated reports. Only group administrators can execute this command.
Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report leaderboard. Shows either the configured value or indicates default value (10) is being used.

pages.title: Project Configuration | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot project configuration commands. Covers setting project metadata including name, short and long descriptions, logo upload, and project URLs. These settings customize how your project appears in reports and on the xbot.ninja public dashboard for discovery by investors and partners.

codeExamples.description: Sets the project name that appears in all generated reports and on the website dashboard

codeExamples.code: /set_project_description A revolutionary DeFi platform for community governance
codeExamples.description: Sets a short 1-2 sentence project description for reports and website display
Configures project links including website and social media URLs for the dashboard

useCases: Brand community reports with official project name instead of generic Telegram group names
Create a professional project profile on xbot.ninja for investor discovery
Provide comprehensive project information to potential partners viewing dashboard
Upload project logo to ensure consistent branding across all generated reports
Link all project resources (website, Twitter, Discord, GitHub) from public dashboard

implementationSteps.title: Set Project Identity
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_project_name to define your official project name and /set_project_description for a concise summary that appears in reports.

implementationSteps.title: Add Detailed Information
implementationSteps.description: Run /set_project_long_description to provide comprehensive project details including mission, features, roadmap, and team for the public dashboard.

implementationSteps.title: Upload Branding Assets
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_project_logo to upload your project logo (PNG, JPG, or GIF) that will appear prominently in reports and on the website.

implementationSteps.title: Configure Project Links
implementationSteps.description: Add all relevant URLs with /set_project_urls including website, social media, documentation, and community links for easy access from the dashboard.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Project Configuration hashtag /set_project_name Sets your project or community name that will appear in all generated reports, leaderboards, and on the website dashboard. This is the main identifier for your community and should be your project's official name or brand. Use this to customize reports with your project's identity instead of using generic Telegram group names. Admin Only Copy /set_project_name CryptoDAO Community Copy /set_project_name My Awesome Project Parameters: Name Type Example project_name required My Project hashtag /get_project_name Displays the current project name configured for your community. Use this to verify what name will appear in reports and on the website, or to check if a project name has been set yet. Admin Only Copy /get_project_name hashtag /set_project_description Sets a short description of your project that appears in reports and on the website. This should be a concise summary of what your project does or your community's mission (1-2 sentences). Use this to provide context about your project's goals when sharing reports with investors, partners, or community members. Admin Only Copy /set_project_description A revolutionary DeFi platform for community governance Parameters: Name Type Example description required value hashtag /get_project_description Displays the current project description configured for your community. Use this to review the short description that appears in reports, or to check if a description has been set. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_long_description Sets a detailed, comprehensive description of your project for the website dashboard. Unlike the short description, this can be multiple paragraphs and include more in-depth information about your project's mission, features, roadmap, and team. This appears on your public dashboard page to provide visitors with complete project details. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example long_description required value hashtag /get_project_long_description Displays the current detailed project description configured for the website dashboard. Use this to review the comprehensive description that visitors see on your public dashboard page, or to check if a long description has been set. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_logo Uploads your project logo that will appear on reports and the website dashboard. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. Send your logo as a photo or file (PNG, JPG, or GIF recommended). The logo should be square or rectangular and will be displayed prominently in all generated reports and on your public dashboard page. Admin Only hashtag /get_project_logo Displays the URL of your currently configured project logo. Use this to verify which logo will appear in reports and on the website, or to share the logo URL with team members. If no logo is set, you'll receive a message prompting you to upload one with /set_project_logo. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_urls Sets important links for your project including website, social media, documentation, and other resources. These URLs appear on the website dashboard as clickable links, making it easy for visitors to connect with your project across different platforms. You can include your website, Twitter/X, Discord, Telegram, GitHub, documentation, or any other relevant links. Separate multiple URLs with spaces or commas. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example urls required https://myproject.com hashtag /get_project_urls Displays all project URLs currently configured for your community. Use this to review which links appear on the website dashboard, verify URLs are correct, or check if any URLs have been set. pages.title: Raid Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot raid coordination commands. Explains how to launch coordinated community raids on X posts, track real-time progress, configure default raid settings, customize raid behavior, and set up automatic raids for daily top tweets. Raids help amplify important posts and drive community engagement.

codeExamples.description: Launches a raid on a specific X post with target engagement goals for likes and retweets
Configures default raid settings including engagement targets and duration for future raids
Enables automatic raids on the best tweet of the day with specified targets and duration

useCases: Coordinate community raids to boost visibility of important project announcements
Support partner projects by organizing engagement campaigns on their key posts
Automatically raid the top daily tweet to consistently amplify best community content
Track real-time raid progress to motivate community participation and celebrate achievements
Configure custom raid images and behavior to match community culture and preferences

implementationSteps.title: Launch a Raid
implementationSteps.description: Use /raidx with a post URL and optional engagement targets (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks) and duration to start a coordinated community raid.

implementationSteps.title: Monitor Raid Progress
implementationSteps.description: Run /raid_status to view real-time progress showing current engagement numbers versus targets, and celebrate when goals are met.

implementationSteps.title: Configure Raid Defaults
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_raid_defaults to set standard engagement targets, duration, and auto-mute behavior for all future raids.

implementationSteps.title: Enable Auto-Raids
implementationSteps.description: Configure /auto_raid_config to automatically raid the best tweet of the day after daily report generation with specified targets and delay.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Raid Commands hashtag /raidx Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. Community members receive a raid announcement with the target post and engagement goals (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks). The bot tracks progress in real-time and celebrates when targets are met. Raids help amplify important posts, support partners, or drive visibility for project announcements. Admin Only Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 likes=50 retweets=20 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 duration=1h mute=yes Parameters: Name Type Example post_url required https://x.com/username/status/123456 likes optional 50 retweets optional 20 replies optional 50 quotes optional 50 bookmarks optional 50 duration optional 1h mute optional yes hashtag /raid_status Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. targets for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and bookmarks. You can check a specific raid by ID or see the latest active raid. Use this to monitor how your community is progressing toward raid goals and motivate participation. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example raid_id optional value hashtag /raid_stop Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. Use this when you need to end a raid early - for example, if the target post was deleted, targets were already met naturally, or the raid was started by mistake. The raid status will be marked as "CANCELLED" in the raid history. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example raid_id required value hashtag /raid_history Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. Review past raid performance to see which raids were successful, how long they took, and engagement levels achieved. Useful for analyzing community participation patterns and planning future raids. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example limit optional value hashtag /set_raid_defaults Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior, and whether raids are enabled. These defaults are used when you start a raid without specifying custom parameters. Set these once to match your community's typical raid goals, then override them per-raid when needed with /raidx parameters. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example likes optional 30 retweets optional 15 replies optional 10 quotes optional 5 bookmarks optional 20 duration optional 1h mute optional no enabled optional yes hashtag /get_raid_defaults Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration, auto-mute behavior, and raid enable/disable status. Use this to review your configured defaults before starting a raid or to verify settings after using /set_raid_defaults. Admin Only hashtag /set_raid_message_behavior Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. Choose between three modes: "edit" (updates the existing message cleanly), "repost" (deletes and resends to keep it at the bottom of the chat), or "pin" (updates and pins the message to keep it visible at the top). Different modes suit different community preferences and chat activity levels. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mode required value hashtag /get_raid_message_behavior Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. Use this to verify how raid progress messages will be displayed in your chat. Admin Only hashtag /set_raid_image Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. This branded image helps make raid announcements more visually appealing and recognizable to your community. The image is displayed prominently in every raid message. Admin Only hashtag /auto_raid_config Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. When enabled, the bot automatically starts a raid on the top-performing post a few minutes after the report is published. This helps consistently boost your best content without manual raid creation. Configure raid duration, delay before starting, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute behavior. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example enabled optional no duration optional 360 delay optional 5 likes optional 100 retweets optional 50 replies optional 50 quotes optional 50 bookmarks optional 50 mute optional yes hashtag /get_auto_raid_config Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after report, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute setting. Use this to review your auto-raid setup or verify changes after using /auto_raid_config. paragraphs: Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. Community members receive a raid announcement with the target post and engagement goals (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks). The bot tracks progress in real-time and celebrates when targets are met. Raids help amplify important posts, support partners, or drive visibility for project announcements.

Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. targets for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and bookmarks. You can check a specific raid by ID or see the latest active raid. Use this to monitor how your community is progressing toward raid goals and motivate participation.

Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. Use this when you need to end a raid early - for example, if the target post was deleted, targets were already met naturally, or the raid was started by mistake. The raid status will be marked as "CANCELLED" in the raid history.

Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. Review past raid performance to see which raids were successful, how long they took, and engagement levels achieved. Useful for analyzing community participation patterns and planning future raids.

Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior, and whether raids are enabled. These defaults are used when you start a raid without specifying custom parameters. Set these once to match your community's typical raid goals, then override them per-raid when needed with /raidx parameters.

Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration, auto-mute behavior, and raid enable/disable status. Use this to review your configured defaults before starting a raid or to verify settings after using /set_raid_defaults.

Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. Choose between three modes: "edit" (updates the existing message cleanly), "repost" (deletes and resends to keep it at the bottom of the chat), or "pin" (updates and pins the message to keep it visible at the top). Different modes suit different community preferences and chat activity levels.
Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. Use this to verify how raid progress messages will be displayed in your chat.

Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. This branded image helps make raid announcements more visually appealing and recognizable to your community. The image is displayed prominently in every raid message.

Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. When enabled, the bot automatically starts a raid on the top-performing post a few minutes after the report is published. This helps consistently boost your best content without manual raid creation. Configure raid duration, delay before starting, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute behavior.

Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after report, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute setting. Use this to review your auto-raid setup or verify changes after using /auto_raid_config.

pages.title: Reports | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot report generation commands. Covers viewing the most recent leaderboard report and manually triggering immediate report regeneration with latest X data. Reports include visual charts, engagement statistics, and performance rankings based on configured scoring rules and tracked accounts.

codeExamples.description: Displays the most recently generated leaderboard report with engagement statistics and rankings
Triggers immediate generation of a fresh report by fetching latest X data
Generates a new report and deletes all existing report history to start fresh

useCases: Share latest community performance data with stakeholders outside scheduled report times
Generate updated report after adding new accounts or keywords to tracking configuration
Create fresh report for presentations or investor meetings on demand
Reset report history when starting a new tracking period or campaign
Verify tracking configuration is working correctly by manually generating test report

implementationSteps.title: View Latest Report
implementationSteps.description: Run /report command to display the most recently generated leaderboard report including visual charts and engagement statistics.

implementationSteps.title: Generate Fresh Report
implementationSteps.description: Use /recreate to manually trigger immediate report generation with latest X data, bypassing the scheduled report timing.

implementationSteps.title: Reset History (Optional)
implementationSteps.description: Add delete-history parameter to /recreate command to clear all existing report history and start tracking fresh from the new report.

pages.title: Schedule Management | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot schedule management commands. Explains how to configure automatic report generation schedules using simple daily time format or advanced cron expressions, view current schedule settings, and delete scheduled automation. Schedules create EventBridge rules for automated report generation at specified times.

codeExamples.description: Sets daily automatic report generation at 12:30 UTC
Schedules reports at 09:00 UTC on specific days of the month (1st and 7th)
Configures reports to generate at 14:30 UTC on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays

useCases: Schedule daily reports at peak community activity times for maximum engagement
Configure weekly reports on specific days for team performance review meetings
Set up monthly reports on the first day of each month for investor updates
Use advanced cron expressions for complex scheduling like weekdays-only reports
Automate report generation to maintain consistent community engagement tracking

implementationSteps.title: Set Report Schedule
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_schedule with HH:MM format for daily reports or include day parameters for weekly/monthly scheduling. Time must be in UTC format.

implementationSteps.title: Verify Schedule Configuration
implementationSteps.description: Run /get_schedule to display the current scheduled time and confirm automatic report generation is configured correctly.

implementationSteps.title: Remove Schedule (If Needed)
implementationSteps.description: Execute /delete_schedule to remove automated report generation and delete the EventBridge rule if scheduling is no longer needed.

paragraphs: Sets a schedule for when reports should be automatically generated. Supports both simple daily time scheduling (HH:MM format) and advanced cron expressions for complex schedules. Creates an EventBridge rule for scheduled report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command.
Retrieves and displays the current scheduled time for automatic report generation. Shows the configured UTC time or indicates if no schedule is set.
Removes the configured daily schedule for automatic report generation. Deletes the EventBridge rule and database entry. Only group administrators can execute this command.

pages.title: Subscription & Credits | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot subscription and credit management commands. Covers accessing Stripe customer portal for subscription management, initiating PRO subscription purchases, checking credit usage, and handling payment success/cancellation callbacks. FREE tier uses credits per report while PRO offers unlimited usage.

codeExamples.description: Opens Stripe customer portal for managing subscription, billing history, and payment methods
Initiates purchase process for X Bot Professional subscription with Stripe checkout
Displays current credit usage and availability based on license type (FREE or PRO)

useCases: Upgrade from FREE to PRO subscription for unlimited report generation and credits
Manage subscription billing and payment methods through Stripe customer portal
Monitor credit consumption on FREE tier to plan upgrade timing
Update payment information or cancel subscription through self-service portal
Track credit usage to understand report generation costs and frequency

implementationSteps.title: Check Current Credits
implementationSteps.description: Run /get_credits to view remaining credits on FREE tier or confirm unlimited usage on PRO subscription.

implementationSteps.title: Upgrade to PRO
implementationSteps.description: Execute /buy command in group chat to start PRO subscription purchase process, which continues in private messages with Stripe integration.

implementationSteps.title: Manage Subscription
implementationSteps.description: Use /subscription command to access Stripe customer portal for billing history, payment method updates, and subscription cancellation options.

pages.title: Topic Management | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for X Bot topic management commands. Explains how to configure forum topic (thread) destinations for bot reports in Telegram groups using Topics feature, and how to verify current topic configuration. Helps organize bot reports in dedicated topics instead of cluttering main chat.

codeExamples.description: Configures the current forum topic as the destination for all bot reports and updates
Displays which forum topic is currently configured to receive bot reports

useCases: Organize bot reports in dedicated forum topic to keep main chat focused on discussions
Create separate topics for different report types or tracking scenarios
Keep analytics and leaderboards separate from general community conversation
Configure topic-based organization for large communities using Telegram Forums feature
Verify report destination topic to ensure reports appear in the correct thread

implementationSteps.title: Navigate to Target Topic
implementationSteps.description: Open the specific forum topic (thread) in your Telegram group where you want bot reports to be sent.

implementationSteps.title: Configure Topic Destination
implementationSteps.description: Run /set_topic command inside the desired topic to set it as the destination for all future bot reports and updates.

implementationSteps.title: Verify Configuration
implementationSteps.description: Use /get_topic to confirm which forum topic is configured and ensure reports will be sent to the correct thread.

pages.title: X Bot Installation | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive installation guide for X Bot covering three setup paths: Crypto Project Setup for tracking project engagement, Individual KOL Setup for showcasing personal metrics, and KOL Team Setup for tracking team performance. Includes step-by-step instructions, verification procedures, and troubleshooting for common issues.

codeExamples.description: Basic setup commands to track official project account and token cashtag
Schedule automated daily reports at 12:00 UTC for regular community updates
Force initial report generation to verify tracking configuration is working correctly

useCases: Track crypto project's X engagement and run community competitions with transparent leaderboards
Build public KOL portfolio on xbot.ninja to showcase performance metrics to potential clients
Monitor KOL team performance across multiple accounts and demonstrate ROI to clients
Verify authentic community engagement for investment due diligence
Organize community engagement campaigns with automated reporting and gamification

implementationSteps.title: Add Bot to Telegram Group
implementationSteps.description: Search for @BWS_X_Bot in Telegram, add it to your group, and make it an admin with required permissions.

implementationSteps.title: Configure Tracking
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_accounts to track X accounts, /add_cashtags for token monitoring, and /add_keywords for topic tracking based on your use case.

implementationSteps.title: Schedule Automated Reports
implementationSteps.description: Set up daily report generation with /set_schedule command using UTC time when your community is most active.

implementationSteps.title: Verify Installation
implementationSteps.description: Run /recreate to generate first report, verify tracking is working correctly, and ensure all configured accounts and keywords appear in results.

implementationSteps.title: Customize and Brand
implementationSteps.description: Add project metadata with /set_project_name, /set_project_description, and /set_project_logo to customize reports and enable website listing.

You can customize tracking later. paragraphs: This guide will walk you through installing and configuring X Bot for your Telegram group. The setup process differs based on your use case — choose the path that matches your needs.
Before installing X Bot, ensure you have:
Admin access to a Telegram group (or ability to create one)
An X (Twitter) account you want to track
Select the setup guide that matches your use case:
Goal: Track your project's X engagement and community activity (optionally use for competitions, prizes, or airdrops)
Add the bot to your project's main Telegram group
Make the bot an admin with the following settings:
Admin rights required: Yes
Special permissions needed: None
The following is a simple example for first-time installation. You can customize tracking later.
Track your official X account:
Track your project's cashtag:
Tracks all posts from your project's X account
Collects engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions)
Includes tracked items in leaderboard rankings
Set up automatic daily analytics reports:
Generates automated reports daily at 12:00 UTC
Tracks top accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions
Keeps community engaged with regular updates
Tip: Choose a time when your community is most active for maximum engagement.
Force an initial report to verify everything is working:
Creates an immediate performance report based on your configured tracking
Confirms tracking is working correctly
Gives you a preview of daily automated reports
Displays your project on the xbot.ninja website for investor and community discovery
Showcases your project's performance metrics publicly
Personalize reports with custom branding.
Set Custom Point Weights (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Bookmarks, Impressions):
Open Telegram and create a new group
Name it something like "My X Analytics" or "KOL Performance Tracker"
This can be a private group for personal tracking
Tip: Keep this group private or share it with potential clients to showcase your metrics.
Add the bot to your newly created group
Admin rights required: Yes
Special permissions needed: None
Monitor your personal X account performance:
Tracks all your posts and engagement metrics
Builds a verifiable portfolio of your performance
Enables discovery on xbot.ninja
Provides data for client reports
Track keywords relevant to your content:
Shows which topics you're covering
Tracks community engagement with your topics
Helps projects find you based on relevant keywords
Get daily performance insights:
Sends daily performance reports at 09:00 UTC
Helps identify top-performing content
Provides data for client reporting
Generate your first analytics report:
Creates an immediate snapshot of your performance
Verifies tracking is working correctly
Gives you shareable proof of your influence
Displays your KOL profile on xbot.ninja
Showcases engagement metrics and expertise publicly
To get publicly listed:
Maintain a minimum performance threshold by posting and engaging consistently
Build authentic engagement (avoid vanity metrics)
Monitor daily reports to track progress toward listing
Note: Featured KOLs are continuously monitored to maintain quality standards.
Goal: Track team performance accurately and show clients real, measurable results
Open Telegram and create a new group
Name it "KOL Team Analytics" or "[Team Name] Performance Tracker"
This will be your shared analytics dashboard
Tip: Make this group private and invite only team members and trusted clients.
Add the bot to your team group
Admin rights required: Yes
Special permissions needed: None
Invite all KOL team members to the group:
Click the group name to open group info
Tap "Add Members"
Select all team members
Ensure everyone can see the analytics and reports
Add all team members' X accounts for tracking:
Tracks team members' posts and engagement
Creates team-wide leaderboards
Enables performance comparisons
Provides aggregate metrics for client reports
Monitor client projects with cashtags:
Tracks team engagement with client tokens
Shows which members promote which projects
Demonstrates ROI to clients with verifiable metrics
Identifies top performers per campaign
Set up daily team performance reports:
Generates daily reports at 10:00 UTC
Tracks trends for each project
Provides regular updates for team and clients
Tip: Schedule for team standups or planning meetings.
Generate your first team analytics report:
Creates an immediate team performance snapshot
Verifies all accounts are tracked correctly
Provides shareable data for client presentations
Displays your KOL team on xbot.ninja
Showcases team engagement metrics publicly
Personalize reports with custom titles:
Hide internal/test accounts from leaderboards:
The bot should appear as an admin in your group
Ensure accounts and keywords are tracked correctly
Wait for your scheduled time to verify automation
Bot not made admin in the group
Bot was removed or banned
Network connectivity issues
Verify bot has admin status
Remove and re-add the bot
Check Telegram server status
Accounts or keywords not configured
Tracking period too short (wait a few hours)
X accounts have privacy restrictions

Insufficient data for report generation
Installation takes just a few minutes. Once configured, X Bot runs automatically, providing scheduled analytics to help you track, measure, and improve your X engagement.

pages.title: X Bot Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of X Bot, a Telegram-based X (Twitter) analytics platform providing trusted metrics directly from the official X API. Explains the dual-purpose platform serving project owners, KOLs, and investors with features including engagement tracking, performance scoring, automated reporting, and curation for quality assurance on xbot.ninja marketplace.

It enables tracking, analysis, and reporting of X activity directly within Telegram groups. X analytics you can trust X Bot uses the official X API to ensure accuracy and reliability. We tested several cheaper alternatives and even built our own "posts crawler," but none matched the accuracy of the official API. Many third-party analytics tools you find online show up to 50% discrepancies in their metrics. With X Bot, you get data directly from the source you can trust. hashtag The Problem X Bot Solves In the crypto space, it's challenging to: Get a trusted measure of community engagement and the real impact of hired KOLs' profitability Identify projects with genuine community traction vs. artificial engagement Find and hire KOL teams that deliver actual results Track and measure authentic X (Twitter) engagement Verify influencer performance with transparent metrics Discover legitimate investment opportunities backed by real community activity X Bot solves these problems by providing verifiable X analytics that distinguish authentic engagement from vanity metrics. hashtag Key Features hashtag Analytics & Tracking Track X (Twitter) accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Monitor engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Generate automated scheduled reports with performance insights (up to 12-hour frequency) View leaderboards ranking top performers by engagement hashtag Performance Scoring X Bot uses a scoring algorithm that weighs different engagement types based on their value. The weights are fully configurable to match each project's specific needs and priorities. Example scoring formula (weights can be customized): Example Engagement Weights Explained: Likes (×1): Basic engagement, shows content appreciation Retweets (×2): Amplification - user shares content with their followers Replies (×1.5): Active conversation starter Quotes (×3): High-value engagement - adds commentary while sharing Bookmarks (×2): Content worth saving for later reference Impressions (×0.001): Reach metric - how many people saw the post Example Calculation: For a post with: 50 likes, 10 retweets, 8 replies, 3 quotes, 15 bookmarks, 5,000 impressions hashtag Customization & Branding Custom point weights for different engagement types Branded colors for visual reports Custom titles for leaderboard sections Project metadata (name, logo, description, URLs) Named filters for different tracking scenarios hashtag Automated Reporting Schedule daily reports at specific times (UTC) Automatic performance screenshots Leaderboards showing top accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and paragraphs: X Bot is a Telegram-based analytics platform that provides trusted X (Twitter) analytics for crypto communities. It enables tracking, analysis, and reporting of X activity directly within Telegram groups.
In the crypto space, it's challenging to:
Get a trusted measure of community engagement and the real impact of hired KOLs' profitability
Identify projects with genuine community traction vs. artificial engagement
Find and hire KOL teams that deliver actual results
Verify influencer performance with transparent metrics
Discover legitimate investment opportunities backed by real community activity
X Bot solves these problems by providing verifiable X analytics that distinguish authentic engagement from vanity metrics.

Generate automated scheduled reports with performance insights (up to 12-hour frequency)

For a post with: 50 likes, 10 retweets, 8 replies, 3 quotes, 15 bookmarks, 5,000 impressions

pages.title: Platform Fees | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Explains BWS platform fee structure and billing model. Covers how blockchain transaction fees are managed, including free testnet transactions, real-time token conversion for mainnet operations, and the $BWS token requirement. Details the blockchain credit fee system with 0.01 USD minimum billable units.
useCases: Calculate costs for creating 100 NFTs on Polygon blockchain
Test blockchain solutions for free using Mumbai or Sepolia networks
Monitor monthly blockchain credit usage and billing
Understand real-time USD to network token conversion costs
Budget blockchain integration projects with transparent pricing
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right Fees Calculator API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Platform Fees We take care of everything. Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”. circle-info Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized. Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience. hashtag Fees Calculator Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience. circle-check In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use. If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks. You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free. We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged. Using test networks does not consume $BWS tokens. Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details, You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free. When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit. Every solution requires $BWS tokens to execute and fund the developer's community and platform maintenance. Please read the platform White Paper arrow-up-right to know more about the $BWS token and its tokenomics. hashtag Pricing Example Suppose you subscribe to Blockchain Web Services and want to create 100 new NFTs on the Polygon blockchain using BWS.NFT.zK arrow-up-right to create the NFTs, and BWS.IPFS.Upload arrow-up-right to save your images to IPFS. Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you: Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan. hashtag How do I pay those Fees? To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage. circle-info BLOCKCHAIN CREDIT FEE We use the Blockchain Credit Fee as the minimum billable unit, 0.01 USD. If, for example, you spend 2 USD creating NFTs, we will add 200 blockchain credit fee units to your next bill. Credits Usage balance. Previous Quick Start chevron-left Next API How Tos chevron-right Last updated 2 months ago Fees Calculator Pricing Example How do I pay those Fees? sun-bright desktop moon Copy 100 IPFS Images, included = 0 USD 100 NFTs x 0.02 Network Fee = 2 USD sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: We take care of everything.
Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”.

Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized.
Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience.
Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience.

In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use.
If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks.
You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free.
We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged.
Using test networks does not consume $BWS tokens.
Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details,
You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free.

When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit.
Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you:
Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan.
To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage.

pages.title: Quick Start | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Quick start tutorial demonstrating how to write 'Hello World' to the blockchain using BWS API. Shows the complete workflow including API authentication, making the first blockchain write operation using BWS.Blockchain.Hash, and checking job status asynchronously to retrieve transaction receipts and Certificate of Trust.

codeExamples.description: Write 'Hello World' to Matchain blockchain using BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution
JavaScript example using jQuery to save data to blockchain
Fetch blockchain job status and results using the returned jobId

useCases: Write first data to blockchain without Web3 wallet management
Test blockchain integration with simple API calls
Retrieve blockchain transaction receipts and verification certificates
Learn asynchronous blockchain operation workflow
Verify data integrity using Certificate of Trust

implementationSteps.title: Get API Key
implementationSteps.description: Obtain your API key for authentication. All API requests require an API key or will return an error.

implementationSteps.title: Write Data to Blockchain
implementationSteps.description: Call the BWS API with solution 'BWS.Blockchain.Hash', specify network (matchain), operation (insert), and provide key-value parameters. Receive a jobId in the response.

implementationSteps.title: Check Job Status
implementationSteps.description: Use the jobId from previous call to fetch job completion status. The blockchain operation is asynchronous, so poll until completed to receive the blockchain receipt and Certificate of Trust.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Quick Start Writing "Hello World!" to the blockchain in just one call! hashtag Get your API key Your API requests should be authenticated using your API key (any request that doesn't include an API key will return an error). circle-info Learn how to Get your API Key . hashtag Your First BWS API Request We're going to use the BWS.Blockchain.Hash arrow-up-right solution to save a message to the blockchain. hashtag Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call Let's write "Hello World!" to the Matchain blockchain network without managing network fees or Web3 wallets by calling a regular Web2 API. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Hash version * number 1 network * string matchain operation * string insert parameters * JSON { key: "mykey", value: "Hello World!" } Please copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your API Key). curl Javascript Phyton If executed correctly, check for jobId in the response body part. We will use it to verify that the blockchain operation has been completed correctly and to receive the blockchain transaction receipt and Certificate of Trust . API Call response hashtag Check for Status To check for job completion and results, use the jobId you got from the previous call. circle-info Please note that interacting with blockchain(s) is asynchronous, so it may take some time for the operation to finish and for the blockchain receipt to be received. hashtag Fetch for the blockchain job status and results. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/fetch Using the previous call response jobId we fetch the blockchain job started previously. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description jobId * String (use previously returned jobId, as for example 59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e) Copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your own API Key). curl Javascript Phyton You should receive a response indicating the completed job and the related blockchain receipt and certificate. Fetch API Call Response Congratulations! In just one call, you used BWS to save data into the Web3 blockchain ecosystem. Previous Welcome! chevron-left Next Platform Fees chevron-right Last updated 2 months ago Get your API key Your First BWS API Request Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. Check for Status Fetch for the blockchain job status and results. sun-bright desktop moon Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }' Copy var settings = { "url": "https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call", "method": "POST", "timeout": 0, "headers": { "X-Api-Key": "API-KEY", "Content-Type": "application/json" }, "data": JSON.stringify({ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }), }; $.ajax(settings).done(function (response) { console.log(response); }); Copy import http.client import json conn = http.client.HTTPSConnection("api.bws.ninja") payload = json.dumps({ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }) headers = { 'X-Api-Key': 'API-KEY', 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } conn.request("POST", "/v1/call", payload, headers) res = conn.getresponse() data = res.read() print(data.decode("utf-8")) Copy { "statusCode": 200, "statusMessage": "", "info": { "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" } } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" }' Copy var settings = { "url": "https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch", "method": "POST", "timeout": 0, "headers": { "X-Api-Key": "API-KEY", "Content-Type": "application/json" }, "data": JSON.stringify({ "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4512-8aca-51d06f69f25e" }), }; $.ajax(settings).done(function (response) { console.log(response); }); Copy import http.client import json conn = http.client.HTTPSConnection("api.bws.ninja") payload = json.dumps({ "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4512-8aca-51d06f69f25e" }) headers = { 'X-Api-Key': 'API-KEY', 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } conn.request("POST", "/v1/fetch", payload, headers) res = conn.getresponse() data = res.read() print(data.decode("utf-8")) Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "status": { "Value": "completed" }, "request": { "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }, "hash": "0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8", "txnUrl": "https://matchscan.io/tx/0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8", "certificateOfTrust": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/bws-backoffice-website-infra-prod/tx/matchain/0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8.pdf", "certificateOfTrustJPG": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/bws-backoffice-website-infra-prod/tx/matchain/0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8.jpg", "certificateOfTrustUrl": "https://prod.bws.ninja/tx.html?t=0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8", "type": { "value": "blockchain-job" }, "timestampInMillis": 1736418605359, "id": "a89bf8bf-392b-4cc3-8ded-7dbe9d43c341" } } sun-bright desktop moon

Select operation retrieves previously saved data using the hash key. Both operations are asynchronous and return jobId for status checking.

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive overview of BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution that simplifies blockchain data storage using hash database methodology. Explains hash database concepts, demonstrates client data management use cases, and highlights benefits including blockchain immutability, transparency, and Certificate of Trust generation. Lists available blockchain networks with contract addresses.
useCases: Store client information using unique client IDs as hash keys
Manage customer profiles with blockchain-verified integrity
Quick data retrieval for reporting and auditing purposes
Ensure transparency of transaction records on blockchain
Provide clients with Certificate of Trust for data verification

implementationSteps.title: Store Client Information
implementationSteps.description: Use a unique client ID as the hash key to link to stored client details or references on the blockchain.

implementationSteps.title: Data Retrieval
implementationSteps.description: Quickly access data associated with a hash key for reporting, auditing, or verification purposes using the select operation.

implementationSteps.title: Enhanced Trust

BWS.Blockchain.Hash simplifies saving data to the blockchain, enabling you to use it as a hash database while abstracting the complexities of blockchain infrastructure. hashtag What is a Hash Database? A hash database is a type of database that uses hash keys to efficiently retrieve and store data. Each record is assigned a unique key, often derived from the data or predefined attributes, which acts as an identifier for quick lookups. Hash databases are known for their speed and scalability, making them ideal for applications requiring rapid data access. hashtag An example: saving client data to the blockchain. Businesses can use BWS.Blockchain.Hash to manage client data by storing essential information (e.g., customer profiles or transaction records) in a blockchain-based hash database. The system uses unique keys, such as client IDs or transaction numbers, to retrieve data effortlessly. Here's how this could work: Client Information Use a unique client ID as the hash key to link to stored client details or references. Data Retrieval Quickly access data associated with a hash key for reporting, auditing, or verification purposes. Enhanced Trust Leverage blockchain’s immutable nature to ensure the integrity and transparency of stored records. hashtag Why Choose BWS.Blockchain.Hash? This solution is perfect for individuals and businesses that want to take advantage of blockchain as a trusted and efficient hash-based storage system. With BWS.Blockchain.Hash , you can store and retrieve data securely, gain the benefits of blockchain transparency, and provide clients with a Certificate of Trust for each entry—ensuring their data is handled responsibly and verifiably. hashtag Available Networks circle-exclamation We're currently updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon (main) and Mumbai (test). Please use Matchain. pages.title: Operations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Complete API reference for BWS.IPFS.Upload operations including upload and list methods. Upload operation accepts JSON or base64-encoded files (images, PDFs) up to 5MB, returning IPFS CID and access URIs. List operation retrieves uploaded files by timestamp range with their CIDs, file types, and access URLs.

codeExamples.description: Upload an image to IPFS using Node.js by encoding the file as base64 and calling BWS API
Upload JSON content directly to IPFS using cURL, returning IPFS CID and access URIs
List IPFS files uploaded within a specific timestamp range, showing CIDs, file types, and access URLs

useCases: Upload NFT images to IPFS for token metadata
Store PDF certificates on decentralized storage
Save JSON metadata files for NFT collections
List previously uploaded IPFS files by date range
Access files via IPFS gateway URLs for web applications

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Content
implementationSteps.description: Optimize content to be less than 1MB (hard limit 5MB). For files, encode as base64 string. For JSON, pass directly or as stringified string.

implementationSteps.title: Upload to IPFS
implementationSteps.description: Call BWS API with solution 'BWS.IPFS.Upload', operation 'new', and provide description and content parameters. Receive IPFS CID and access URIs in response.

implementationSteps.title: Access Uploaded Files
implementationSteps.description: Use returned IPFS URI (ipfs://) or web URL (https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/) to access the file. Optionally list files by timestamp range to retrieve upload history.

POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the IPFS CID and useful URIs. Copy { " statusCode " : 200 , " info " : { " cid " : " QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN " , " uris " : { " ipfs " : " ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN " , " url " : " https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN " } } } hashtag Upload to IPFS Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption description string Short description of your content (will be returned when listing your IPFS files ). content JSON / base64 Content to save on the IPFS network. We currently support: JSON (just pass the JSON or the stringified string) File sent as a base64 encoded string circle-exclamation Please note that when uploading your file: You must optimize your content to be less than 1 MB in size (there is a hard limit of 5MB when uploading files to IPFS using our API). We currently support JSON, images, and PDF files. If your use case requires higher file sizes or other file types, please contact us. hashtag Upload to IPFS Examples Upload an Image using nodejs Upload JSON using cURL hashtag Upload to IPFS call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the IPFS Content Identifier (CID) along with the corresponding IPFS URI and URL. These details facilitate easy access and reference to the uploaded content on the IPFS network. hashtag List IPFS Files Get the list of your IPFS files POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string list parameters JSON check Method Parameters 200 The list of files you uploaded to IPFS hashtag List IPFS Files Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption from long Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files from. to long Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files to. hashtag LIST IPFS Files Request Example cURL hashtag List IPFS Files call Response Once executed correctly, you will get a list of the IPFS uploaded files using BWS API. That list will contain for each file: the timestamp of creation ( Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds), the IPFS Content Identifier (CID), the file type, and the URIs to easily access the file IPFS URI, web URL - using the BWS IPFS Gateway to fetch the file. A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: XqaLg...729v' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "new", "parameters": { "description": "Just a simple JSON for testing BWS API", "content": { "text": "Hello World!" } } }' Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "cid": "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } Copy { "statusCode": 200, "statusMessage": "", "info": [ { "": "" } ] } Copy curl --location 'https://api.staging.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: XqaLg...729v' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "list" "parameters": { "from": 1700404140000 "to": 1700411940000 } }' Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": [ { "timestamp": "1700416540067", "cid": "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "description": "Just a simple JSON for testing BWS API", "fileType": "json", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } }, { "timestamp": "1700121600009", "cid": "QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu", "description": "image for token 1325", "fileType": "image", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu" } } ] } sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS.
Use this operation to upload content to IPFS and get the IPFS Content Identifier (CID).

Content to save on the IPFS network.
We currently support:
JSON (just pass the JSON or the stringified string)
File sent as a base64 encoded string
Please note that when uploading your file:
You must optimize your content to be less than 1 MB in size (there is a hard limit of 5MB when uploading files to IPFS using our API).
If your use case requires higher file sizes or other file types, please contact us.

When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the IPFS Content Identifier (CID) along with the corresponding IPFS URI and URL. These details facilitate easy access and reference to the uploaded content on the IPFS network.
Get the list of your IPFS files
Once executed correctly, you will get a list of the IPFS uploaded files using BWS API. That list will contain for each file:
the IPFS Content Identifier (CID),
web URL - using the BWS IPFS Gateway to fetch the file.

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the BWS IPFS Upload service for storing images, PDFs, and JSON files on the decentralized IPFS network. Explains how IPFS provides permanent, decentralized storage with content addressing and describes the BWS IPFS gateway (ipfs.bws.ninja) for accessing uploaded files.
useCases: Store NFT metadata and asset images for blockchain applications
Host decentralized content delivery for logos and graphics
Share structured JSON data in a transparent and verifiable way
Upload PDF documents to permanent decentralized storage
Create immutable content with unique content identifiers (CIDs)
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. BWS IPFS API Service leverages the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) , a distributed, peer-to-peer storage protocol designed to create a permanent and decentralized method of storing and sharing data. IPFS breaks files into smaller chunks, distributes them across the network, and assigns each file a unique content identifier (CID) for efficient retrieval. hashtag BWS IPFS Gateway Use ipfs.bws.ninja as the gateway to access the files you have uploaded to IPFS using BWS. circle-info An IPFS gateway acts as a bridge between the decentralized IPFS network and traditional web browsers, allowing users to access content stored on IPFS using standard HTTP protocols. This makes IPFS content easily accessible without requiring specialized software or configuration. Check out our lovely unicorn on IPFS using the BWS Gateway: https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmU7avmnTb4iVbNGCHMJmdEbsx9nRtUx7dWGpKeE7zjo8T arrow-up-right hashtag Key Features of IPFS Decentralized Storage Files are stored across multiple nodes, enhancing reliability and fault tolerance. Content Addressing Data is retrieved using its CID, ensuring that files are immutable and tamper-proof. Global Accessibility IPFS data can be accessed by any node in the network, fostering collaboration and resilience. hashtag Supported File Types Our service supports uploading images. PDF and JSON files , making it ideal for use cases where these formats are predominant. Examples include: NFT Metadata and Assets Storing JSON metadata files and associated images for blockchain applications. Decentralized Content Delivery Hosting static resources like logos or graphics for web or app projects. Data Integrity and Provenance Sharing structured data (e.g., JSON) in a transparent, verifiable way. pages.title: NFT Attributes (traits) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Explains NFT attributes (traits) and how they define unique characteristics for non-fungible tokens. Shows metadata structure with trait_type and value pairs, using an animated cat NFT collection as an example with traits like fur color, eye type, and accessories.

codeExamples.description: Example NFT metadata structure showing how attributes define unique traits for a digital cat collectible

useCases: Define unique characteristics for digital art collections
Create rarity tiers for NFT collections with trait variations
Build metadata for animated character NFTs with multiple attributes
Specify accessory traits for collectible NFT series
Structure NFT metadata for marketplace display and filtering
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark ⌘ Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down BWS API Docs Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Solution Overview chevron-right Operations chevron-right NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right chevron-up chevron-down gitbook Powered by GitBook xmark block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK NFT Attributes (traits) Making your NFT unique. Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) represent unique digital assets on blockchain platforms. Those assets can define associated traits , which are specific attributes or characteristics that detail each NFT. In a practical scenario, an NFT's metadata might look something like this for a digital art collection of animated cats: Copy { " name " : " Animated Cat #001 " , " description " : " A unique animated cat with distinct features. " , " image " : " ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu " , " attributes " : [ { " trait_type " : " Fur Color " , " value " : " Black " }, { " trait_type " : " Eye Type " , " value " : " Round " }, { " trait_type " : " Accessories " , " value " : " Hat " } ] } Within the metadata, the attributes array contains the various traits of the NFT. Each trait is defined by a trait_type , which specifies the category of the trait (e.g., "Fur Color" or "Rarity"), and a value , which indicates the specific characteristic of that trait (e.g., "Black" or "Ultra-Rare"). Traits encapsulated within this metadata not only bring out the uniqueness of each token but can also influence its perceived value, especially if some traits are considered rarer than others. BWS.NFT.zK allows you to create an NFT by passing those traits . circle-exclamation Please note that it's the usage of those traits when visualizing your NFT that you must take care of and validate. For example, if you use the provided Certificate of Trust for your users' experience use case, you must check they are correctly displayed. Previous Operations chevron-left Next Blockchain Badges chevron-right Last updated 2 months ago sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon
