pages.title: Operations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Complete API reference for BWS.NFT.zK operations including Create NFT, List NFTs, Transfer NFT, and Send NFT by Email. Provides detailed request/response examples, method parameters, and explanations of asynchronous operations with job IDs for blockchain transaction tracking.

codeExamples.description: API call to create (mint) a new NFT on the Mumbai test network with name, description, image URL, and rarity trait
Response from NFT creation API call returning a job ID for tracking the asynchronous blockchain transaction

useCases: Mint new NFTs on Polygon or Ethereum blockchain networks
List and retrieve all NFTs created through your BWS account
Transfer NFT ownership to a blockchain wallet address
Send NFTs to users via email who don't have crypto wallets
Track NFT creation and transfer transactions using job IDs

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT
implementationSteps.description: Make a POST request to /v1/call with solution 'BWS.NFT.zK', operation 'new', and parameters including name, description, image URL/IPFS URI, and optional attributes

implementationSteps.title: Track Job Status
implementationSteps.description: Use the returned jobId with the fetch API to check blockchain transaction status and retrieve NFT details including transaction hash, IPFS hashes, and transfer codes

implementationSteps.title: Transfer or Send NFT
implementationSteps.description: Use the 'transfer' operation with wallet address or 'send' operation with email address to transfer ownership, then fetch results using the new jobId

Check the blockchain transaction status and receipt using the fetch API and the provided jobId. hashtag Creates a new NFT. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create (Mint) a new NFT on a selected blockchain Network. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Copy { " statusCode " : 200 , " statusMessage " : "" , " info " : { " jobId " : " 59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e " } } hashtag Create NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Name of your NFT. description string The NFT description. image URL (or IPFS URI) This is the URL or IPFS URI of your NFT image. attributes optional JSON These are the attributes of your NFT. hashtag Create NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Create NFT Fetch Response chevron-right Create NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag The fetch operation , once the NFT creation job is completed, will return the following: Parameter Type Description guid string NFT unique id network string Network the NFT has been created in. nftTxHash string Blockchain transaction hash nftId string NFT id nftIPFSHash string NFT metadata file IPFS hash nftImageIPFSHash string NFT image file IPFS hash nftJson json NFT metadata json url json (check NFT Urls ) status string The NFT status: "created" or "transferred" transferCodes json (check Transfer Codes ) hashtag NFT Urls hashtag Easy-to-use URLs for you to get access to NFT's most relevant data. Parameter Type Description nft URL NFT metada URL image URL NFT image URL transaction URL NFT blockchain transaction explorer URL hashtag Transfer Codes hashtag Transfer codes that are required to transfer ownership when sending an NFT through email (check NFT Ownership to learn more). Parameter Type Description owner string Owner code is required to execute a transfer when sending an NFT over email. receiver string The receiver code the new owner should indicate to execute the NFT transfer. hashtag List NFTs hashtag Lists your NFTs. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Get the list of your NFTs. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK operation * string list parameters JSON check Methods Parameters 200 Returns the list of your NFTs. hashtag List NFT Method Parameters circle-info If no range is defined, the last 20 NFTs are returned. Parameter Type Desciption from long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs from. to long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs to. hashtag List IPFS Files call Response hashtag LIST NFT Request Example cURL hashtag List NFT Response The list operation returns the list of your NFTs, including NFT-relevant data and the network you created the NFT in. chevron-right List NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag hashtag Transfer NFT circle-info This operation is asynchronous. (once executed, check the results using fetch API and the provided jobId) hashtag Transfer an NFT ownership. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Transfer an NFT ownership by using the new owner's wallet or email address. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string transfer parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id hashtag Transfer NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. address string Wallet address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) hashtag Transfer NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Transfer NFT Fetch Response The fetch operation for the NFT transfer API call will mainly state if the transfer has been successful ( completed ) and the related costs. chevron-right Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag hashtag Send NFT by Email hashtag Send an NFT by email. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Send an NFT by using the new owner's email address. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string send parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id hashtag Send NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. email string Email address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) hashtag Send NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Send NFT by Email Response As a confirmation message response, if the NFT send is confirmed you will get your NFT-related details, including the codes that the new owner will have to use to execute a blockchain NFT transfer. chevron-right Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag Previous Solution Overview chevron-left Next NFT Attributes (traits) chevron-right Last updated 2 months ago Create NFT Creates a new NFT. paragraphs: BWS.NFT.zK API Operations.
Use this operation to create (Mint) a new NFT on a selected blockchain Network.

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive guide to NFT.zK solution covering ownership models, transfer methods, and data storage. Explains how to transfer NFTs to wallet addresses or email addresses, how NFT images and metadata are stored on IPFS, and lists available blockchain networks including Matchain, Polygon, and Ethereum.

codeExamples.description: Example API call JSON showing NFT creation with a URL for the image, which will be automatically uploaded to IPFS
Example NFT metadata structure using IPFS URI for the image, showing how metadata is stored on IPFS

useCases: Transfer NFT ownership to users without Web3 wallets via email
Create NFTs with custody managed by BWS account before transfer
Store NFT images on IPFS from web URLs or existing IPFS URIs
Deploy NFTs on Matchain, Polygon, or Ethereum networks
Enable non-crypto users to receive and claim NFT ownership

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT with BWS Custody
implementationSteps.description: Use BWS.NFT.zK to create a new NFT which will initially be linked to your BWS account with custody managed until transfer

implementationSteps.title: Choose Transfer Method
implementationSteps.description: Transfer ownership either to a blockchain wallet address using the Transfer NFT operation, or to an email address for users without Web3 wallets

implementationSteps.title: NFT Image and Metadata Upload
implementationSteps.description: Provide NFT image as web URL or IPFS URI - BWS automatically uploads to IPFS if needed and creates metadata file on IPFS pointing to the image

paragraphs: When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options:
Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address.
Transfer ownership to an email address.
Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account.
If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address.
We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to.
Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address.
To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as:
a web addressable URL, or,
as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki).
API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image.
In all the scenarios, your NFT metadata will point to an image stored in IPFS, as we will upload your image to IPFS if required.
When a new NFT is created, we create and upload the NFT metadata to IPFS,
